content.problemStatement:
In this lesson, you'll learn about common bug patterns that can appear while programming in Java.
- Identifying these patterns will help you debug your code effectively and enhance your programming skills.
- As you tackle debugging, remember to keep your code organized using comments (from the "Comments and Basic Best Practices" module).
- Reading and understanding error messages can also guide you in identifying the source of a bug.

---

**Common Bug Patterns**:
1. **Off-by-One Errors**:
```java
// The loop iterates through the string, but 'i+1' causes an index to go out of range on the last iteration
String text = "ABCD";
for (int i = 0; i  0) {
   System.out.println(x);  // Infinite loop because x is never updated
}
```
5. **Index Errors**
```java
String text = "123";
System.out.println(text.charAt(5));  // StringIndexOutOfBoundsException: String index out of range: 5
```
